mod.name: Closed Loop Ecology
mod.description:
Realistic carbon and nitrogen cycles for Space Haven.
Vanilla's life-support economy has three structural breaks: - Composting biomass loses 87% of its mass to the accounting void - Carbon is mineable but gated at Mining skill 7 (peer with Noble Metals) - Chemicals → Fertilizer can only be made from Rot, which requires corpses
Peaceful playthroughs can't close the food loop without trading.
This mod ships five surgical patches:
1. Carbon mining skill 7 → 2 (matches Base Metals); Raw Chemicals 6 → 3. Carbonaceous chondrite asteroids become early-game accessible.
2. Composter carbon retention ×6 (1 biomass → 0.10 carbon, matches real biochar yields of 30-70%).
3. CO2 scrubber rebalanced to the exact mathematical inverse of the carbon burner. The C ↔ CO2 round-trip is now mass-conservative — no more free-carbon exploit from looping burner + scrubber.
4. New recipe — Industrial Chemicals Synthesis (interactive at the existing Chemical Refinery): 1 Carbon + 3 Water → 3 Chemicals. Real-world analog: Fischer-Tropsch synthesis. Closes the fertilizer cycle without combat dependency. Conditional "produce if storage < X" condition works because outputs are item-only.
5. New recipe — Biomass Pyrolysis (interactive at Chemical Refinery): 2 Biomass → 1 Carbon. Real-world analog: industrial pyrolysis / charcoal production. Bypasses the lossy composter for high-throughput carbon refining.
Gas byproducts (oxygen from electrolysis, CO2 from pyrolysis) are intentionally not modeled — they would break the conditional UI on interactive recipes. Reserved for a future Pyrolysis Reactor station that can host both the interactive recipe and its non-interactive gas-vent on its own (without polluting vanilla stations).
After install, a peaceful 5-crew ship can run an indefinite food cycle on the carbon they mine + the biomass they compost. Water remains the primary bottleneck (ice mining), as in vanilla.
mod.author: mingc
